key_insights.insight: The voice in your head, the ego, is not you; it's simply a set of thoughts that don't need to be believed or acted upon.
key_insights.quote: You can have all the thoughts and the chatter going, but you don't have to believe it and you don't have to act on it. You just simply listen to it.

key_insights.insight: It's essential to get quiet and look at your thoughts to identify underlying self-opinions and understand who you truly are.
key_insights.quote: The only way you will really understand who you are and hear that voice is by getting quiet... You're getting to know yourself. God, I think this, I didn't even know I was thinking that you know it just spills out of you

key_insights.insight: Being authentic involves a daily practice of self-compassion and challenging negative thought patterns that don't serve you.
key_insights.quote: You have to stay dedicated to being nice to yourselfand not letting these mental patternsstop your creativityor stop you trying new thingsor stop you putting yourself out there

key_insights.insight: Systemic societal issues can make it difficult for certain groups to feel like they fit in, highlighting the importance of both personal and societal change.
key_insights.quote: People in the disability community, if you can't get into a shop because there isn't a ramp there,where do you feel you fit into society?

key_insights.insight: The real meaning in life is found in connection, both with others and with something larger than oneself.
key_insights.quote: For looking at the bigger everything noticing that I'm on a floating ball in space noticing that all of this is changing always

key_insights.insight: It's important to detach mentally from comparing yourself to others in social settings, realizing that these comparisons aren't reality.
key_insights.quote: So I think I just try and extract myself mentally from it meaning too much and just go you know like I say like some it's undulating...

key_insights.insight: Women often face unique challenges in openly discussing their ambitions and success due to societal judgments and expectations.
key_insights.quote: For women, that's quite a new thing...But I do think it's still seen as a different choice for women to say,I'm very ambitious.

key_insights.insight: Listen to that internal voice when making important life decisions and understand that others may not always agree.
key_insights.quote: They won't often bebacked up by everyone around you, but if you know it's right, that little voicewill only get louder and louder.

actionable_advice.advice: Engage in daily journaling or writing to explore thoughts and feelings and gain self-awareness.
actionable_advice.how_to: Set aside time each day to write in a diary, or use a dictaphone to record thoughts and feelings, focusing on whatever comes to mind without judgment.

actionable_advice.advice: Practice self-compassion by identifying and challenging negative self-talk patterns.
actionable_advice.how_to: When noticing a pattern of workaholism or self-criticism, ask yourself 'what is this that I don't like about myself?' and make a conscious effort to choose actions that promote self-care, such as going for a walk.

actionable_advice.advice: Engage in non-religious prayer or express gratitude before bed.
actionable_advice.how_to: Before going to sleep, give thanks for the positive aspects of the day and for overall well-being, and then express hope and support for others.

actionable_advice.advice: Practice walking meditation or spend time in nature without distractions.
actionable_advice.how_to: Go for a walk in nature without a phone, focus on the surroundings, and become aware of thoughts and feelings.

actionable_advice.advice: Measure your actions by 'how you feel' instead of quantifiable metrics
actionable_advice.how_to: Consider that the absence of negative feelings like disconnection and boredom are a 'return on investment' when choosing actions.

memorable_stories: Burning old diaries as a symbolic act of starting anew after a period of depression.
Experiencing the first panic attack on the motorway and the subsequent journey of understanding and managing anxiety.
Fern's mother writing a piece about her own depression within 10 minutes of being asked.
contrarian_claims: It's okay to turn up to professional situations not feeling great; authenticity is more valuable than pretending to be happy.
Rather than banishing the ego and negative thoughts, it's better to listen to them, acknowledge them, but not believe or act on them.
That as much as we are tiny in the grand, grand scheme of things, that we are supported by all of this greatness.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Fearne Cotton: THIS Is How To Build Confidence & Set Yourself Free
